options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage <number_of_cycles> # Number of cycles allowed for the path

options.name: -end
options.description: Multicycle adjustment is to be applied relative to the ending clock edge for the path

options.name: -fall
options.description: Apply the assertion at the falling edge of the signal on the path endpoints

options.name: -fall_from
options.description: List all multicycle paths originating from the from_list at falling edge

options.name: -fall_through
options.description: Applies the assertion at the falling edge of the signal on the through pins

options.name: -fall_to
options.description: List all multicycle paths originating from the to_list at falling edge

options.name: -from
options.description: List all multicycle paths originating from the from_list

options.name: -hold
options.description: Applies multicycles for hold times only

options.name: -rise
options.description: Applies the assertion at the rising edge of the signal on the path endpoints

options.name: -rise_from
options.description: List all multicycle paths originating from the from_list at rising edge

options.name: -rise_through
options.description: Applies the assertion at the rising edge of the signal on the through pins

options.name: -rise_to
options.description: List all multicycle paths originating from the to_list at rising edge

options.name: -setup
options.description: Applies multicycles for setup and affects the hold check. It does not change the hold multiplier

options.name: -start
options.description: Multicycle adjustment is to be applied relative to the starting clock edge for the path

options.name: -through
options.description: Applies the assertion to both the rising and the falling edges

options.name: -to
options.description: List all multicycle paths originating from the to_list
